input.content:
# Working Agreement — Outbid Dirigent

**Last Reviewed:** 2026-04-20
**Next Review:** 2026-07-20 (quarterly)

## Team Context

Outbid Dirigent is maintained by a solo owner (Jonah Kresse) working alongside
AI agents (Claude Code, subagents). Many traditional working-agreement sections
assume a multi-engineer team — this document adapts them to a solo-maintainer-
plus-agents context. The agreement still applies: it governs how work gets from
idea to merged code, regardless of whether the co-worker is human or an agent.

## Meeting Cadences

| Meeting | Frequency | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Self-retro | Monthly | Review what shipped, what broke, what agent behaviours need CLAUDE.md updates |
| Quarterly review | Quarterly | Re-run `/assess`, update this agreement and `CLAUDE.md`, audit stale memory |

Standups, sprint planning, and refinement are intentionally omitted — not
applicable for a single-maintainer project. If the team grows, reinstate them.

## Communication Norms

- **Issue tracker / backlog:** ClickUp (see user-level memory for project references).
- **PR discussion:** GitHub PR comments.
- **Agent instructions:** `CLAUDE.md` at repo root. If an agent produces
  unexpected output, update `CLAUDE.md` in the same PR that corrects the output
  — not in a follow-up.

## Definition of Done

A change is "done" when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] Code reviewed (human or agent) — no self-merge of feature work without review
- [ ] All tests passing (unit + integration; E2E where applicable)
- [ ] Tests added or updated alongside behaviour changes
- [ ] Structured reviewer invoked (`standards-reviewer` agent on the staged diff)
- [ ] Feature toggled if user-facing and incremental — N/A for this CLI
- [ ] Documentation updated — README, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, ADRs, runbooks as needed
- [ ] If AI-generated code is a significant portion of the change, flagged via
      `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer in the commit message
- [ ] Conventional Commits format used for the commit message
- [ ] If an architectural decision was made, an ADR exists in `harness-docs/adr/`

## On-Call Expectations

**N/A.** Dirigent is a CLI tool and Python package — no production runtime
service, no on-call rotation. If a breaking defect ships to users, the fix
follows the normal DoD process (no emergency shortcut).

If this repo ever grows a hosted component, reinstate on-call norms before
shipping that component.

## Decision-Making

- **Day-to-day technical decisions:** Maintainer's call.
- **Architectural decisions:** Documented as ADRs in `harness-docs/adr/`
  using `/adr`. ADRs are created before or alongside the change that makes
  the decision load-bearing — not after.
- **Deviations from portfolio engineering standards:** Follow the deviation
  protocol in `CLAUDE.md` (comply with the explicit instruction, flag the
  deviation and reason in the PR description).

## Code Review Norms

- Every PR gets at least one review pass before merge — either from a human
  reviewer or from an agent-driven review (e.g. `/bid-harness:code-review`,
  `pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer`, or `standards-reviewer`). Self-merge
  without a review pass is not allowed for feature work.
- Use [Conventional Comments](https://conventionalcomments.org/) style: labels
  (`nit:`, `issue:`, `question:`, `suggestion:`) make intent legible and avoid
  ambiguity.
- Approve with minor comments rather than blocking on style nits.
- Stale PRs (> 3 days without activity) get resolved: merge, revise, or close.
- **AI-generated code:** treat the agent as the author under review. Do not
  rely on the agent to write and self-approve its own tests; verify coverage
  independently.

## Retrospectives

Monthly self-retro covers, at minimum:
- What shipped? What broke?
- Did any agent produce unexpected output? If so, was `CLAUDE.md` updated in
  the same PR that corrected it?
- Any recurring guidance the user had to repeat to agents? If so, save as a
  feedback memory so it sticks.

Retro action items get tracked in ClickUp with an owner (always the
maintainer here) and a due date.

## Review and Update

This agreement is reviewed quarterly. If a change would materially alter how
work is done (e.g. a second maintainer joins, production runtime added,
different agent workflow adopted), update this document in the same PR that
makes the change.

Working agreement at `harness-docs/working-agreement.md`, PR template at `.github/pull_request_template.md`. Section 01 now **3 ✅ / 3 ❌** (was 0 / 6). Grand total: **20 ✅ / 51 ❌**.

### Two shape adjustments from the template

- **On-call: N/A** — dirigent is a CLI/Python package, no production runtime. Documented the reason and the trigger for reinstating it (if a hosted component is ever added).
- **Retrospectives: monthly self-retro** instead of bi-weekly — bi-weekly retros don't fit a solo maintainer. Kept the substance (what shipped, what broke, agent-behaviour updates, CLAUDE.md fixes) and the quarterly re-review of the agreement itself.

### Bonus: Section 03 / Section 10 items the PR template also satisfies

`/verify-assessment` will likely flip these from FAIL/WARN to PASS once re-run, because the template now enforces them structurally:

- Section 03: "PR description includes what/why/test/ticket link" — PR template has all four sections
- Section 03: "Peer review required before merge" — working agreement §Code Review Norms + PR template checklist
- Section 03: "Conventional Comments style feedback" — linked in working agreement
- Section 10: "Flag AI-generated code in commits/PR descriptions" — explicit checkbox group in template
- Section 10 REC: "AI-assisted code review in PR template" — now present

I didn't flip these manually because the skill scoped me to Section 01. Run `/verify-assessment` to reconcile.

### Reminder

Working agreements should be co-created by the team and reviewed quarterly. This one is currently solo-authored — if another engineer joins, run through it together and update the assumptions (standups, communication channel, on-call rotation).

AI agents operating on this repo: plan-approved-then-execute, stop-and-
surface on failure, no silent retries or unauthorised fallbacks.

Block covers:
- Autonomy level (Semi-autonomous) with explicit failure-mode contract
- AI-generated code policy (DoD parity, human-read gate, Co-Authored-By
  trailer)

  of autonomy level
- Prompt-injection awareness (treat external content as untrusted)
- Tool permissions (least privilege, no prod credentials in context)

fail-stop-on-failure / prompt-injection-awareness from FAIL/WARN to

CLAUDE.md-first-class / scope-to-repo / no-secrets-in-context rows.

Section 10: 3 → 7 MUST passes. Grand total: 13 → 17 MUST passes.

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(harness): add working agreement and PR template

Create harness-docs/working-agreement.md via /bid-harness:working-agreement,
adapted for a solo-maintainer-plus-agents context. On-call is N/A (no
production runtime); standups and bi-weekly retros are dropped in favour
of a monthly self-retro focused on what shipped, what broke, and whether
CLAUDE.md needs updates after unexpected agent output. Quarterly review
of the agreement itself.

Add .github/pull_request_template.md to operationalise the DoD: what-why-
how-to-test sections, Conventional Commits check, standards-reviewer
invocation, an AI-generated-code checklist (Co-Authored-By trailer,
human-read gate, independent test-coverage verification, prompt-injection
flag), and a deviation-from-standards section.

Flips Section 01 MUSTs: written Working Agreement / Definition of Done /
retrospectives from FAIL to PASS. Section 01: 0 → 3 MUST passes.
Grand total: 17 → 20 MUST passes, 54 → 51 MUST fails.

The PR template also satisfies several Section 03 and Section 10 items
structurally (PR description schema, flag AI-generated code, AI-review
checklist) — those will flip on the next /bid-harness:verify-assessment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(harness): track portfolio engineering standards and document templates

Commit the bid-harness scaffold that CLAUDE.md already references as
authoritative: the full engineering standards document and the ADR / PIR /
runbook / tracking-plan / working-agreement templates under harness-docs/
templates/.

These files were installed by the bid-harness plugin earlier in the v2
cycle but never added to source control, leaving CLAUDE.md with dangling
references to harness-docs/engineering-standards.md and
harness-docs/templates/*. Tracking them closes the gap so Section 02
(ADR template), Section 09 (PIR template), and the CLAUDE.md §Templates
pointer all resolve against checked-in content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(claude): vendor bid-harness plugin + standards agents into repo

Commit the locally-installed bid-harness plugin tree under
.claude/plugins/bid-harness/ (20 SKILL.md files + plugin.json) and the
two standards-review agents under .claude/agents/ (standards-reviewer,
compliance-checker). These were installed by Claude Code's plugin
manager but remained untracked — meaning the skills and agents that
CLAUDE.md Rule #11 and the /bid-harness:* command table reference only
worked on machines that had independently installed the plugin.

Tracking them makes the /assess → /adr → /working-agreement → etc.
workflow reproducible from a clean checkout (assuming Claude Code runs
the /reload-plugins step). Drift against the upstream bid-harness repo
is the trade-off; accept it for now in exchange for a self-contained
working copy.

Update .claude/settings.json to declare enabledPlugins explicitly and
keep the existing secret-scan PreToolUse hook, Stop-hook DoD nudge, and
Python auto-ruff PostToolUse hook from the harness install.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
